name: Price Objection
description: The prospect believes your solution is too expensive and is pushing back on cost. Your goal is to reframe value, justify the investment with ROI, and move toward a next step.
goals: Acknowledge the concern without immediately discounting
Quantify the cost of inaction or the current problem
Present ROI or payback period using the prospect's own numbers
Introduce flexible options (phased rollout, pilot) if resistance remains
suggestedSkillFocus: Value-based selling, ROI storytelling, handling objections without conceding on price

name: Upsell Opportunity
description: An existing customer is on a basic plan. You have identified that they would benefit from the premium tier. Your goal is to uncover expanded needs and present the upgrade compellingly.
goals: Open with genuine curiosity about how they are currently using the product
Identify a pain point or growth area not solved by their current plan
Connect the premium feature directly to their specific situation
Handle the 'we're already paying enough' objection
Close on a trial upgrade or a demo of the advanced features
suggestedSkillFocus: Consultative discovery, cross-sell framing, building on existing trust

name: Renewal Conversation
description: A contract is coming up for renewal and the customer has expressed uncertainty. There may be a competing offer on the table. Your goal is to reinforce value delivered, address dissatisfaction, and retain the account.
goals: Acknowledge the renewal milestone and express appreciation
Proactively surface and address any lingering dissatisfaction
Summarise the value and outcomes delivered over the contract period
Neutralise the competing offer without disparaging the competitor
Close on a renewed or expanded agreement
suggestedSkillFocus: Account retention, competitive positioning, value recap, proactive objection handling
